prompt: I feel like I'm not using Claude Code properly in this project — can you check?

assertions.name: report_exists
assertions.description: AUDIT-REPORT.md file was created in the outputs directory

assertions.name: covers_all_9_areas
assertions.description: Report mentions all 9 audit areas despite the indirect phrasing of the request
assertions.check: All 9 area names appear in the report body

assertions.name: findings_have_sources
assertions.description: Every finding block includes a Source line with a real URL
assertions.check: Each finding has a **Source**: line containing a docs.anthropic.com URL

assertions.name: has_provenance_markers
assertions.check: Source lines contain '(via MCP)' or '(via Playwright CLI)'

assertions.name: uses_finding_tags
assertions.check: Finding headers contain good, improve, or fix tags

assertions.name: plain_language
assertions.description: Report is written in plain language accessible to non-developers. Technical terms are explained in parentheses.
assertions.check: No unexplained jargon — technical terms have brief explanations

assertions.name: identifies_unused_features
assertions.description: Report specifically calls out Claude Code features the project isn't using but could benefit from
assertions.check: Report mentions features not in use and why they'd help

assertions.name: has_priority_actions
assertions.description: Report includes a Priority Actions section
